2606.20209.title: FlowMaps: Modeling Long-Term Multimodal Object Dynamics with Flow Matching
2606.20209.paper_summary: Joint spatial and temporal understanding of 3D scenes is a crucial requirement for robots deployed in everyday household environments. Such agents must not only comprehend and navigate spatial layouts, but also reason about how these spaces evolve over time. In particular, humans interact with objects daily, causing them to change position throughout the environment and making it difficult for robots to reliably associate current observations with previously seen objects. However, these interactions are not random: human habits and routines induce spatio-temporally consistent patterns in object locations, which robotic agents can potentially learn and then exploit for downstream tasks such as navigation. To this end, we introduce FlowMaps, a latent flow matching model for estimating multimodal distributions over the future locations of dynamic objects in a continuous 3D space. By learning the implicit dependencies among objects and their temporal evolution, FlowMaps predicts likely changes in object locations conditioned on past human interactions, while supporting generalization across previously unseen environments that share similar object routines. To demonstrate the utility of this method, we deploy FlowMaps in a downstream dynamic Object Navigation task in both simulated and real-world environments. Across more than 600 episodes, FlowMaps outperforms state-of-the-art approaches, showing that modeling object dynamics through continuous, multimodal spatio-temporal distributions improves robotic search and navigation in changing household environments. Code and additional material is available at https://fra-tsuna.github.io/flowmaps/.
2606.20209.paper_summary_zh: 聯合空間和時間的理解對於部署在日常家庭環境中的機器人來說是一個至關重要的要求。這些代理不僅必須理解和導航空間佈局，還必須推理這些空間如何隨時間演變。特別是，人類每天都與物體互動，導致它們在環境中改變位置，這使得機器人難以可靠地將當前觀察與之前見過的物體聯繫起來。然而，這些互動並不是隨機的：人類的習慣和日常活動在物體位置上引發了時空一致的模式，機器人代理可以潛在地學習並利用這些模式來進行下游任務，例如導航。為此，我們引入了FlowMaps，一種潛在流匹配模型，用於估計在連續3D空間中動態物體未來位置的多模態分佈。通過學習物體之間的隱含依賴關係及其時間演變，FlowMaps預測基於過去人類互動的物體位置可能的變化，同時支持在共享類似物體日常活動的先前未見環境中的泛化。為了展示這種方法的實用性，我們在模擬和現實環境中的下游動態物體導航任務中部署了FlowMaps。在超過600個回合中，FlowMaps的表現超過了最先進的方法，顯示通過連續的多模態時空分佈建模物體動態能夠改善在變化的家庭環境中的機器人搜索和導航。代碼和其他材料可在 https://fra-tsuna.github.io/flowmaps/ 獲得。
2606.20209.author: Francesco Argenziano et.al.
2606.20209.authors: Francesco Argenziano, Miguel Saavedra-Ruiz, Sacha Morin, Charlie Gauthier, Daniele Nardi, Liam Paull
